name: Security Scanner
description: Scans the PR diff for hardcoded secrets, vulnerable dependencies, injection vectors, and insecure patterns. PII scrubbing is applied to the entire diff before every LLM call — any detected secrets are replaced with [REDACTED:TYPE] before leaving the process.

checks.pass: 📦 package.json present — dependency audit can proceed
checks.fail: ⚠️  package.json not found — skipping dependency CVE check

checks.prompt: You are a security engineer reviewing a pull request diff. Analyse the changes for: (1) hardcoded secrets or API keys, (2) SQL/command injection vectors, (3) insecure direct object references, (4) missing authentication checks, (5) vulnerable package version bumps. For each finding output: severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), file path, line range, description, and remediation. Context: {retryContext}
checks.fail: ⚠️  Security scan returned no structured output

checks.prompt: Review the security findings list and eliminate false positives. Confirm each CRITICAL and HIGH finding is real and actionable. Output the final confirmed findings with one-line remediation per issue.
